-- 1 of 4 --

PLC Overview
!
Now I'm going to talk about prelaunch content. We're focused specifically on the
internal launch and everything we cover here can go for the JV launch. It's put together
the same way.
!
Module four we talked about the prelaunch and then later on we're going to be talking
about the open cart where we are actually selling stuff but this is the prelaunch. Now,
this is basically that sideway sale letter that we talked about in module one.
In here, you're going to have three pieces of prelaunch content. We've got prelaunch
content one, two, and three and these days in general they're done via video. They
don't have to be done via video but usually they are these days. The examples that I'm
going to be giving you are video based. The reality is that these days video is easy to
do even if you aren't comfortable on camera, you don't have to be on camera, you can
see screen cap video, no problem. You've seen how I've used some video of me being
in front of the camera but also playing screen cap video. You can do it that way if you
don't want to be in front of the camera.
It's generally easier to be more authentic and more engaging on video than it is in
writing. There are so many of us that once we start writing we think you have to sound
like a college professor or something. In any case, video is what's working these days
so we're going to focus on video here.
!
I'm going to walk you through these examples and you're going to see, we do the
sideway sales letter and that problem solution path, that's P as path.
!
That's where you come out and you define a problem and then you give the solution
and then inherent in that solution there's often another problem. Then you give the
solution for that one in your second video and then inherent in that there's usually a
third problem here. Then you solve that problem as well but then there's always
another problem and that's going to get solved in your sales letter, your sales video.
That's the problem solution path.
!
You can also look at it as the opportunity solution where you're showing opportunity
and then you give the solution or how to get there opportunity and then that often
develops into another opportunity and you saw that one as well and you move through
giving opportunities and solutions all the way through.
Very similar as the problem solution path. One example I'm going to give you is Will
Hamilton who teaches tennis instructions. The opportunity is what he's really going to
teach and you could look at it as a problem or as an opportunity.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module5v1.0

-- 2 of 4 --

It's like a glass that's half full, that's half empty, what Will is teaching is a course on
tennis strategy. The opportunity is you don't have to spend hours and hours in the
weight room or hours on the court to improve your game. If you sit there and watch
the videos and learn about strategy you can improve your game that way as well.
!
He delivers that big time opportunity and then he talks strategy and defines another
opportunity or sort of a problem within that and then another problem. Eventually it's
like, okay I've taught you all these strategies but then how you actually use those once
you get into a match and you have to react to what the other person is doing is the
next step. You could see how he masterfully does this presenting opportunities or
problems all the way through leading into the sale.
!
There's another thing we talked about, mental triggers, back in module two. We went
through all the mental triggers like authority, likeability, reciprocity, scarcity, community,
social proof, anticipation, interaction. You're going to see as you go through these
examples how these are built in.
!
Often like in this very first piece you have to develop some real authority, some real
positioning for yourself. You have to position yourself as someone that the viewer
should pay attention to. You do that right away or else they're not even going to watch
your video.
You're going to see there's a lot of different ways to create that authority. Going back to
that sideway sales letter, the first video is generally about defining an opportunity for
some type of transformation, some type of change, some type of result.
!
Whether they're going to lose weight, find the love of their life, make more money, cut
some strokes off their golf score, or learn how to bake an amazing cake. There is some
type of opportunity where you really start to make that transformation real for them.
Show them how the transformation can be absolutely real and that second video is
often the most flexible. Frankly it's often the weakest video. Generally, you want to
open really strong with a really strong video. You want to close really strong and if
you're going to have a weak one, it's going to be the middle one but that's a hidden
secret. Not every video is perfect or fantastic or awesome. If I look at my at my launch
videos, I'll often say, "Well that one was really great. That one was okay." We kept our
head above water and then we killed it with this one.
Frankly, a lot of times it's that second video that doesn't completely crush it. I want you
to crush every single one of your videos, I hope you do. I'm going to do my best to
teach you how to do that but reality is reality. We don't all hit a home run every single
time. You're going to see that as we go through these videos. I'm going to point out the
good and the bad. Remember there is flexibility in here.

-- 3 of 4 --

One of these examples actually uses four videos. It's like, "Oh my gosh, four video jobs.
Is that break the launch?" It doesn't break out, three is the standard but that's what I
teach. It's possible to do four. There's been plenty of times where I've done four. There
is flexibility in them all. Remember there is an overall story arc here.
!
In Will Hamilton's launch about his tennis ninja launch, the overall story arc is that idea
of the pros. What makes the pros win at the top isn't their strokes, I've got great
strokes, I've got great fore hands, back hands, great serves. It's strategy that wins.
Strategy is something you can get better at even without spending more time on the
tennis court. In the middle of the winter, when you can't play tennis, you can sit there
in front of your computer and improve your team just by learning strategy. That's the
overall art here and that PC hits a strong here but then you can watch through different
portions of strategy and different ways to put it to use. Eventually how do you actually
bring it into your game here within your matches. You'll see how this overall story arcs
but then breaks it down and sees different opportunities, different problems, and
different solutions.
That's your overall prelaunch content and I know you're going to love it as we go
through these examples and I'll break it down minute by minute, second by second of
exactly what's happening in these videos.
